rationale: The cited paper is used for more than a passing literature-list mention. The citing article repeatedly draws on it to frame socially desirable responding as involving both conscious and unconscious components, including a specific discussion of this point in a methodological footnote and again in interpreting respondent behavior. It also cites the paper when defining and using impression management versus self-deceptive enhancement in the nomological validation section. These uses indicate a meaningful conceptual support role for the citing paper's setup and interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Steenkamp et al. (2010) as conceptual grounding for socially desirable response tendencies, especially the conscious versus nonconscious bias distinction and IM/SDE framing. This background supports how the new dual-questioning method is motivated and how underreporting results are interpreted.
